gcSpeech.title: Message from GC ቤንሃናን
gcSpeech.subtitle: A word of blessing to the graduating class
gcSpeech.speech:
Dear beloved graduates of 2026,

As you step into a new chapter of life, remember the foundation laid here in fellowship — in prayer, in worship, and in service. You have been shaped not only by your studies but by the love of this community.

Go forth with courage, carry Christ in your hearts, and let your light shine wherever you go. The fellowship family is proud of each one of you.

May God bless you abundantly.

With love,
GC ቤንሃናን

home.completeProfile: Complete your profile to appear in the graduate gallery.
home.berketSpeech:
"The Lord is a friend to those who fear him; he makes his covenant known to them."
— Psalm 25:14

To our 2018 graduating class, GC Benhanans, may the Lord help you in all things. This website was prepared for GC Benhanans, where all our graduation memory photos and final words are gathered.

To our brothers and sisters, the 2018 Batch 14 and Batch 15 graduates, may the Lord help you again. God has shown his faithfulness in everything; we lack nothing, for the Lord who helped and sustained us in all things—to him alone be glory.

"You will all remain in our hearts, together with your memories, as we have loved you."

GC Benhanan loves you. ❤️
home.semagagnSpeech:
Beloved brothers and sisters of our graduating class, it has been one of the greatest privileges of my life to walk with you through this season of preparation, worship, and fellowship at Arba Minch University Christian Fellowship. I have watched you grow in prayer, in service, and in love for one another, and I have seen how the Lord has faithfully shaped each heart for the paths that lie ahead. As you leave these halls, do not leave behind the foundation we built together: the habit of seeking God early, the joy of gathering as one family, and the courage to shine Christ's light in every workplace, every home, and every community you enter. Remember the songs we sang, the conferences we shared, the trainings that stretched us, and the quiet moments when God's presence was real among us. Carry those memories not as nostalgia alone, but as proof that you are never alone, for the same God who sustained us here will go with you. Wherever you are sent, serve humbly, love generously, stay connected to one another, and remain rooted in the Word. The fellowship family believes in you, prays for you, and will always welcome you home. May the Lord bless you, keep you, and use your lives for his glory.

In Christ,
Semagegn
